codeExamples.python:
import requests

base_url = 'http://localhost:3010'
path = '/auth/register'
url = f'{base_url}{path}'

response = requests.post(
    url,
    json={
      "email": "verify-test-1778896444508@example.com",
      "password": "TestPassword123!"
    }
)
data = response.json()
print(data)
codeExamples.emojicode:
📦 sockets 🏠

💭 Emojicode HTTP Client
💭 Compile: emojicodec example.🍇 -o example
💭 Run: ./example

  3010 ➡️ port

  💭 Build HTTP request
  🔤POST /auth/register HTTP/1.1❌r❌nHost: localhost:3010❌r❌nContent-Type: application/json❌r❌nContent-Length: 79❌r❌n❌r❌n{"email":"verify-test-1778896444508@example.com","password":"TestPassword123!"}🔤 ➡️ request

  💭 Connect and send
  🍺 🆕📞 host port❗ ➡️ socket
  🍺 💬 socket 📇 request❗❗
  
  💭 Read and print response
  🍺 👂 socket 4096❗ ➡️ data
  😀 🍺 🔡 data❗❗
